county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Milwaukee County formed 1834 from Brown County. County seat is Milwaukee, Wisconsin's largest city. Major industrial and brewing center. 'German Athens' -- very strong German heritage. Also major Polish, Irish, Italian, and African American communities. Catholic Archdiocese of Milwaukee.
county_metadata.research_tips: Milwaukee County Register of Deeds holds land records from 1834. Wisconsin's most populous county and largest city with excellent archival resources. The Milwaukee County Historical Society has extensive manuscript, photograph, and map collections. Milwaukee Public Library Frank P. Zeidler Humanities Room has genealogy collections. University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee Archives Department holds labor, ethnic, and urban records. WHS has microfilmed Milwaukee County records. VERY strong German immigrant heritage -- Milwaukee was called the 'German Athens.' Also major Polish, Irish, Italian, and African American communities. Brewing industry records (Schlitz, Pabst, Miller, Blatz) document workers and families. Milwaukee city directories from 1847. Catholic Archdiocese of Milwaukee archives hold extensive parish registers. Naturalization records at NARA Great Lakes Region. FamilySearch has extensive Milwaukee County collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Milwaukee County Register of Deeds online access; historical deed books from 1834 at county office and WHS microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Milwaukee County Register of Deeds, Milwaukee
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Milwaukee County from 1834; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1834; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Milwaukee County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Milwaukee County Probate Court, Milwaukee
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Milwaukee County from 1834; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Milwaukee County Clerk, Milwaukee / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Milwaukee County from 1834; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Milwaukee County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Milwaukee County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Milwaukee County German Church Records -- Catholic / Lutheran Parishes
source_profiles.repository: Local parishes / Catholic Archdiocese of Milwaukee or Diocese archives / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) for Milwaukee County; many parish registers kept in German until the 1900s; Catholic records through archdiocese/diocese archives; Lutheran records (Missouri Synod, Wisconsin Synod) through church archives; WHS has transcribed some German church records; essential for German immigrant family research

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Milwaukee County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Monroe County formed 1854 from La Crosse County. County seat is Sparta. Western Wisconsin county with Fort McCoy military installation. Norwegian, German, and Irish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Monroe County Register of Deeds in Sparta holds land records from 1854. Western Wisconsin county with Fort McCoy military installation -- military records document many families. Norwegian, German, and Irish immigrants settled here. The Monroe County Local History Room and Museum in Sparta maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Monroe County records. The Deke Slayton Memorial Space & Bicycle Museum. Leon and Cashton area Norwegian settlement. Church records (Norwegian Lutheran, German Lutheran, Catholic) supplement civil records. Camp Douglas/Volk Field records. FamilySearch has some Monroe County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Monroe County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Sparta; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Oconto County formed 1851 from Brown County. County seat is Oconto on Green Bay. Northeastern Wisconsin lumber county. Copper Culture archaeological site. German, Polish, and Scandinavian settlement. Parent county for Florence, Forest, Langlade, and Marinette.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oconto County Register of Deeds in Oconto holds land records from 1851. Northeastern Wisconsin county on Green Bay. One of the oldest continuously occupied sites in Wisconsin (Copper Culture archaeological site). Lumber industry records are important. German, Polish, and Scandinavian immigrants settled here. The Oconto County Historical Society in Oconto maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Oconto County records. Christian Science church founded its first congregation in Wisconsin in Oconto (1886). Copper Culture State Park. FamilySearch has some Oconto County records. BLM GLO patents. Check Oconto County for pre-formation records of Florence, Forest, Langlade, and Marinette counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Oconto County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Oconto; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1851; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Oneida County formed 1885 from Lincoln County. County seat is Rhinelander. Northern Wisconsin Northwoods county with tourism and lumber heritage. Named for the Oneida people (though the Oneida reservation is in Brown/Outagamie counties).
county_metadata.research_tips: Oneida County Register of Deeds in Rhinelander holds land records from 1885. Created from Lincoln County -- check Lincoln County for pre-1885 records. Northern Wisconsin lumbering and tourism county (Northwoods lakes region). German, Polish, and Scandinavian settlers followed the lumber companies. The Oneida County Historical Society in Rhinelander maintains collections. Rhinelander Logging Museum. WHS has microfilmed Oneida County records. Note: this Oneida County is named for the Oneida people but the Oneida Nation reservation is in Brown and Outagamie counties, not here. FamilySearch has some Oneida County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Oneida County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Rhinelander; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Outagamie County formed 1851 from Brown County. County seat is Appleton in the Fox River Valley. Lawrence University. Oneida Nation reservation partly in county. Strong German and Dutch settlement. Paper industry.
county_metadata.research_tips: Outagamie County Register of Deeds in Appleton holds land records from 1851. Fox River Valley county with the Oneida Nation reservation partly within its borders -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Lawrence University archives in Appleton. Appleton was home to Harry Houdini and Edna Ferber. The History Museum at the Castle in Appleton has excellent collections. WHS has microfilmed Outagamie County records. Very strong German and Dutch immigrant communities. Paper mill industry records document workers and families. Appleton city directories from the 1870s. FamilySearch has indexed Outagamie County records. Catholic and Lutheran church records essential.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Outagamie County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Appleton; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ozaukee County formed 1853 from Milwaukee and Washington counties. County seat is Port Washington on Lake Michigan. Very strong Luxembourger immigrant community. Also German and Irish settlement. Port Washington draft riot (1862).
county_metadata.research_tips: Ozaukee County Register of Deeds in Port Washington holds land records from 1853. Created from Milwaukee and Washington counties. Lake Michigan county north of Milwaukee. VERY strong Luxembourger immigrant community -- Port Washington and Belgium, WI have one of the highest Luxembourger concentrations in America. Also German and Irish settlement. The Ozaukee County Historical Society in Cedarburg has collections. WHS has microfilmed Ozaukee County records. Cedarburg has well-preserved historic district. Catholic parish records (Archdiocese of Milwaukee) essential for Luxembourger families. Covered Bridge Park. FamilySearch has some Ozaukee County records. Port Washington draft riot (1862) records at WHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Ozaukee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Port Washington; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pepin County formed 1858 from Dunn County. County seat is Durand. Western Wisconsin Mississippi River county. Wisconsin's smallest county by area. Laura Ingalls Wilder birthplace (1867). Swedish, Norwegian, and German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Pepin County Register of Deeds in Durand holds land records from 1858. Created from Dunn County -- check Dunn County for pre-1858 records. Western Wisconsin Mississippi River county. Laura Ingalls Wilder was born in Pepin (1867) -- the 'Little House in the Big Woods' site. Swedish, Norwegian, and German immigrant settlement. Wisconsin's smallest county by area. The Pepin County Historical Society in Durand maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Pepin County records. The Laura Ingalls Wilder Museum in Pepin. FamilySearch has limited Pepin County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Church records (Lutheran, Catholic) supplement civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Pepin County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Durand; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Pierce County formed 1853 from St. Croix County. County seat is Ellsworth on the Mississippi/St. Croix. Western Wisconsin county. Swedish, Norwegian, and German settlement. 'Cheese Curd Capital of Wisconsin.'
county_metadata.research_tips: Pierce County Register of Deeds in Ellsworth holds land records from 1853. Created from St. Croix County -- check St. Croix County for pre-1853 records. Western Wisconsin county on the Mississippi and St. Croix Rivers. Swedish, Norwegian, and German immigrants were the primary groups. The Pierce County Historical Association in Ellsworth maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Pierce County records. University of Wisconsin-River Falls has some regional archival materials. Ellsworth is the 'Cheese Curd Capital of Wisconsin.' FamilySearch has some Pierce County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Lutheran and Catholic church records supplement civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Pierce County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ellsworth; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Polk County formed 1853 from St. Croix County. County seat is Balsam Lake. Northwestern Wisconsin county. Strong Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish, Danish) settlement. St. Croix Chippewa tribal presence.
county_metadata.research_tips: Polk County Register of Deeds in Balsam Lake holds land records from 1853. Created from St. Croix County. Northwestern Wisconsin county with heavy Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish, Danish) settlement. The Polk County Historical Society in Balsam Lake maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Polk County records. Gandy Dancer State Trail follows the old railroad grade. Inter-County Leader newspaper archives. St. Croix Chippewa tribal presence. FamilySearch has some Polk County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Norwegian Lutheran church records are critical supplements.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Polk County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Balsam Lake; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Polk County Register of Deeds, Balsam Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Polk County from 1853; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1853; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Polk County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Polk County Probate Court, Balsam Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Polk County from 1853; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Polk County Clerk, Balsam Lake / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Polk County from 1853; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Polk County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Polk County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Norwegian Lutheran Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Norwegian-American Historical Association (NAHA), St. Olaf College / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian Lutheran congregation records (baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials) for Polk County; many records in Norwegian; NAHA at St. Olaf College has emigration records and bygdelag (community) records; WHS has some transcribed Norwegian church records; Vesterheim Norwegian-American Museum in Decorah, IA also holds relevant collections

source_profiles.source_name: Polk County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Polk County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Polk County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Portage County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Stevens Point. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Very strong Polish immigrant community. University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point. Lumber industry heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Portage County Register of Deeds in Stevens Point holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Stevens Point was a major lumber industry center. Very strong Polish immigrant community -- Stevens Point is one of the most heavily Polish cities in America. The Portage County Historical Society in Stevens Point maintains collections. University of Wisconsin-Stevens Point archives hold local and regional materials. WHS has microfilmed Portage County records. Catholic parish records (Diocese of La Crosse, esp. Polish parishes) are essential. Polish-language newspaper records at WHS. FamilySearch has indexed Portage County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Portage County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Stevens Point; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses
